name: Stage 32-wide K macro-tiles so each sync feeds two MMA slices

summary.name: Stage 32-wide K macro-tiles so each sync feeds two MMA slices
summary.hypothesis: This fixed shape is exactly divisible by 32 along K, but the current hot path still pays cp.async commit/wait, shared-fragment setup, and CTA handoff overhead every 16-wide K step. Reblocking the main loop so one staged epoch covers two 16x16x16 MMA slices should increase tensor work per scalar/LSU episode, reduce barrier frequency, and raise tensor issue without repeating the round-11 producer/consumer register blow-up.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Tensor-core underfeed from too little MMA per staging/synchronization episode in the fixed-shape main loop.
summary.risk: High. A 32-wide macro-step can raise shared-memory footprint and live fragment state enough to erase the gain if registers or residency move the wrong way.
summary.stop_condition: Stop if tensor activity does not move clearly upward or if the wider K stage materially increases registers/shared memory without beating the restored 43.697664 ms base.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
